Theodore Adams, shot through


the head and permanently para


lyzed by c. U. Smith, passenger
on a street car hey. attempted to


hold up in Spokane, last New
Years eve, is reported failing fast


at his mothers home In Anaconda
Mont. Young Adams was pardon
ed by Governor Hay because of his
condition.


The Inland Empire system on
September 90 will advance the
rates on the Palouse- Moscow
branch from IN to 3 cents per
mile. The communication tickets tc
points 20 miles out will still con.
tinue in force, giving the holder
of the ticket rate Of IN cents per
mile. 1t is claimed that the pas
senger traffic on the Palouse line


has not been paying.


Rev. F. A. Brown has resigned
the pastorate of the Medical Lake
Congregational church and will
move to Hillyard.


Rev. w. J. Hindtey, pastor Of the


Pilgrim Congregational church.
says that until economic conditions


change Sunday base ball must be


tolerated. He says that II he were


to work eight or 10 hours a day


for six days in the week he would


attend Sunday base ball games


himself.
A social will be given tonight
by the imperial lodge of Odd Eel


lows at the l. o. o. F. building at
Wall and Main at which past
grandmaster's jewel will be pre-.


scented to Attorney L. ]. Birdseye
Of Spokane, who recently retired
from the head of the order in the
state. The Imperial quartet, con
sisting of H. A. Elwell, George
Dewey, Tom Heddle and .Larue
Perrine will sing.


Superintendent Watson Of the
city schools In talk to his teach
ers said that it Is not proper for
teachers to be seen smoking on


the streets or going fishing on
Sunday. Superintendent Watson
comes from the east.


The gang employed on the Great
Northern tunnel near the scene OF
Tuesday evenings catastrophe


when J. H. Cook and Roy Davis


lost their lives, have struck be-


cause of fear of a repetition Of the
accident. A new gang has been
sent to work to clean the earth


out of the caved portion of the


tunnel and strengthen the roof
with new timbers.


The Palmer Chemical Co. has


been organized as subsidiary cor.
poration of the Spokane Drug Co.
1t will manufacture various chemi-
Gals and farm and fruit compounds
Present indications are that the


banquet to be held tonight at the


Hall of the Doges in honor Of


Henry George will be well attend.


ed. There will be a number OF


speakers, including Rev. Hfndiey,


Mayor Pratt, President Kegley of


the tSate Grange and others.
A new record for bank clearinos


in Spokane was made in August,


when the total amounted to $780,


694, 20S, or 100000 more than any


preceding month.
